t_ch: 稍微动一点点心思，就能轻易骗过星际和平公司最强大的安保系统。哪怕你是无家可归的流浪汉，只要身份信息检测通过，同样可以坐进他们的贵宾室！
t_en: With just a small amount of effort, you can easily fool the Interastral Peace Corporation's most powerful security systems. Even a homeless person can get into the IPC's VIP lounge as long as they pass the ID verification!

t_ch: 离家出走的天环族，和某个年迈的皮皮西富商…在数据层面可以是同一个人！普通人都能看出来的差别，系统指标却分辨不出，实在是太可笑了。
t_en: A Halovian who ran away from home and an old, wealthy Pepeshi merchant... They could be exactly the same person in terms of data! The fact that system parameters cannot identify differences that any regular person could visually spot is truly laughable.

t_ch: 别用这种看待罪犯的眼神盯着我…要怪，就只能怪他们雇的设计师太蠢，留下这么明显的漏洞，不被人钻空子才奇怪呢！
t_en: Don't look at me like I'm a criminal... If you want to blame someone, blame the foolish designers they hired! It would be strange if people didn't exploit such obvious loopholes!

t_ch: 公司想抓到我可没那么轻松！
t_en: If the IPC wants to catch me, it won't be that simple!

t_ch: …实在没法理解！
t_en: ...I just don't get it!

t_ch: 我打电话给我爸那个老顽固，说打算回匹诺康尼度个长假，结果…结果他居然通知了猎犬家系来酒店抓我！他就是贪图公司给的那笔悬赏金！
t_en: I called my stubborn old man and told him I'd be taking a long vacation back in Penacony. You know what happened...? He informed the Bloodhound Family and told them to come and capture me at the hotel! He wants the bounty that the IPC put out on me!

t_ch: 是啊，我也是犯傻了。我就不该在那个失眠的深夜，把自己做过的事都告诉他。
t_en: You're right. I was being dumb. During that insomniac night, I shouldn't have told him everything I've done.

t_ch: 可我能怎么办…除他以外，我已经没有亲人了。除了匹诺康尼，我也没有能够回去的地方。这里是我最后的牵挂。
t_en: But what am I supposed to do...? I have no family left besides him. I have nowhere to return to except Penacony, either. This is the only place I have ties to.

t_ch: 但我不会束手就擒。既然能从公司的围捕中逃走这么多次，就不怕再多一次！
t_en: But I'm not just going to hand myself in! I've managed to escape the IPC's hunts so many times, I can do it again!

t_ch: 嘿，这位客人，欢迎光临钟表餐厅克劳克影视乐园分厅。请问您是想来点——
t_en: Hey, dear guest! Welcome to the Clock Diner in Clock Studios Theme Park! May I ask what I can get you—

t_ch: 钟表披萨蛋糕卷，百层圣代柠檬挞；\n切片蛋糕飞碟堡，苜蓿色拉苏乐达？
t_en: We have Clockie Pizza, Cake Rolls, Hundred-Layered Sundaes, and Lemon Tarts\nSliced Cakes, Floatdisc Burgers, Alfalfa Salad, or SoulGlad?

t_ch: 软糖酸梦彩梦好，圣代百层薯条佳；\n噼咔汽水兑糖浆，脆筒最配爆米花！
t_en: Sour Dreams and Dreamlight are good, sundaes with fries are the best.\nPika soda and syrup or crispy cones and popcorn beat all the rest!

t_ch: （鼓掌）
t_en: (Applause)

t_ch: 哈哈，谢谢谢谢。
t_en: Haha, thank you, thank you.

t_ch: 口才真好。
t_en: That was a great performance.

t_ch: 哈哈，雕虫小技而已，您谬赞了。
t_en: Haha, it's just a little trick I do, it's not that big a deal.

t_ch: 那么，您准备来点什么呢？
t_en: So, what can I get you?

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 嗯？当然可以，我最喜欢和人聊天啦。不过…客人不如先买瓶苏乐达，咱们边喝边聊，免得干了嗓子？
t_en: Hm? Of course! I love talking to people. Although... How about buying a SoulGlad first and we can drink as we chat, we don't want you getting thirsty!

t_ch: 好啊，给我瓶经典苏乐达。
t_en: Alright, a bottle of Classic SoulGlad.

t_ch: 嘿呀，我就喜欢您这样的客人。来，经典苏乐达一瓶，您拿好！
t_en: Hey! You're the best kind of customer! Here, one Classic SoulGlad.

t_ch: 梦里嗓子不会干。
t_en: You don't get thirsty in a dream.

t_ch: 那您饿了吗？要不来一份薯条圣代，垫垫肚子？
t_en: Then, are you hungry? How about a Potato Fries Sundae to fill your belly?

t_ch: 行吧，那就来一份。
t_en: Alright, I'll take one.

t_ch: 好嘞，薯条圣代一份，您慢慢吃！
t_en: Okay, one Potato Fries Sundae, bon appétit!

t_ch: 梦里也不会饿。
t_en: You don't get hungry in dreams, either.

t_ch: 零食呢？我向您倾情推荐美梦脆筒，就像广告上说的——「您能够品尝到美梦的味道」！
t_en: Then what about a snack? I would recommend the Dreamy Cone! You know, the ad says: "Taste the flavor of your dreams!"

t_ch: 既然你这么努力推销，行吧…
t_en: As you're so intent on selling me something, go on then...

t_ch: 哈哈，客人您可真体贴！相信我，您会喜欢这支「美梦」的！
t_en: Haha, you're so thoughtful! Believe me, you will enjoy this "beautiful dream"!

t_ch: 我什么都不想吃…
t_en: I don't want to eat anything...

t_ch: 嗨，您早说嘛，我也省得耽误您时间…哎呦！
t_en: Hey, then why didn't you say! I wouldn't have wasted your time like this otherwise!

t_ch: 实在是不好意思，我突然想起有点事，下次再陪您聊天啊！
t_en: My apologies, but I just remembered something that I have to do, I'll chat with you next time, okay?

t_ch: 来，客人想聊什么？您问，我必定知无不言！
t_en: What would you like to talk about, dear customer? Ask me anything and I'll tell you all I know!

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 嗯？客人想聊什么？您问，我必定知无不言！
t_en: Hm? What would you like to talk about, dear customer? Ask me anything and I'll tell you everything I know!

t_ch: 为我介绍一下影视乐园吧？
t_en: What can you tell me about the theme park?

t_ch: 这个嘛…「克劳克影视乐园」归属于「克劳克影业」，是关于钟表小子、哈努兄弟、仓鼠球骑士等卡通角色的乐园。
t_en: I see... Clock Studios Theme Park belongs to Clock Studios, it is a theme park all about cartoon characters like Clockie, Brother Hanu, and Hamster Ball Knight.

t_ch: 您可以在此处与扮演它们的皮套演员互动，并游玩与各个卡通形象相关的游艺项目。
t_en: You can interact with costumed performers who are dressed as cartoon characters and enjoy all sorts of entertainment based on these characters.

t_ch: 不过这些信息在梦境护照上就能查到，您想听点不一样的吗？
t_en: But, you can find all of this information on the Dreamscape Pass, do you want to hear about something else?

t_ch: 说吧，又要推销什么…
t_en: Go on, what are you trying to sell me this time...

t_ch: 哈哈，客人您真是上道。要来份「酸梦」软糖吗？便宜实惠、酸爽可口，保证您喜欢！
t_en: Haha, you know your stuff, dear customer. How about some "Sour Dreams" Soft Candy? They're great value for money, sour and refreshing, I guarantee you'll love them!

t_ch: 给您讲个好玩的吧——我有个朋友，原本是个星际驰名的运动健将，光是到手的金牌就能摆满一面墙。
t_en: I'll tell you something interesting... I have a friend who was once a celebrated athlete throughout the galaxy, he could cover a whole wall with the gold medals he's won.

t_ch: 但您猜怎么着，他退役之后竟然不远万里跑来这里当了皮套演员！您知道他有多喜欢钟表小子吗？这系列每一集动画他都看过，厉害吧！
t_en: But, guess what? After he retired, he immediately came running here from far away to start work as a costumed performer! Do you know how much he loves Clockie? He's seen every single episode — Isn't that amazing?

t_ch: 您回头在这边游玩的时候可以稍微留意些，说不定正巧就能遇见他呢。
t_en: You can keep an eye out the next time you're here to have fun — you might just run into him.

t_ch: 算了，我不想听。
t_en: Forget it, I don't want to hear it.

t_ch: 诶呀，真可惜…不过没关系，如果客人您什么时候想听了，尽管来找我就好。
t_en: Oh my, that's a shame... But that's okay! If you want to hear what I have to say sometime, just come and find me.

t_ch: 聊聊你自己怎么样？
t_en: Why not tell me about yourself?

t_ch: 嗨，我有什么可聊的。我就普普通通一逐梦客，放在人群里挑都挑不出来。
t_en: Hey, there's nothing much to say about me. I'm just a plain old Dreamchaser... You couldn't pick me out of a crowd!

t_ch: 倒是您，看着就器宇不凡，您是做哪行的啊？
t_en: But you, one look at you and I know you're someone special. What do you do?

t_ch: 普普通通一无名客。
t_en: I'm just an ordinary Nameless.

t_ch: 放在宇宙里挑都挑不出来。
t_en: You couldn't pick me out of the universe.

t_ch: 哎呦，我就说吧，您果然是个人物。看在咱这交情上，您以后可别忘了多多照顾我的生意啊！
t_en: Oh my, I knew it! You are a big shot! As we're friends now, don't forget to come and help me out with business, okay?

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 没问题，您快去忙您的吧。
t_en: Alright, I'll let you get back to your business.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: …诶呦，没想到竟能得您厚爱，可真是我的荣幸。不过嘛，我两个哥哥可能不太喜欢别人对我这么做。
t_en: ...Oh my, I did not expect you to be so kind. This is truly an honor. Although, my two brothers don't really like people treating me this way...

t_ch: 他们啊，一个是有权有势的本地人，一个是身强体壮的运动健将，我实在不想您因为这点小事和他们起冲突……
t_en: As for them, one is a powerful local figure, the other a strong athlete. I really don't want you to conflict with them over such a trivial matter...

t_ch: 所以您看，要不还是算了吧？
t_en: So, why don't we just forget it?

t_ch: 我想买点吃的。
t_en: I'd like to buy some food.

t_ch: 没问题，您准备来点什么呢？
t_en: No problem, what can I get you?

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's it. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 好嘞，多谢惠顾，下次再来啊！
t_en: Alright, thank you for your patronage, I hope to see you again soon!

t_ch: 客人您又来啦，欢迎欢迎！今天想要吃点什么、喝点什么？
t_en: Dear customer, you're back! Welcome, welcome! What can I get you today? Something to eat? To drink?

t_ch: 让我给您先报个菜单！欢迎光临钟表餐厅克劳克影视乐园分厅。请问您是想来点——
t_en: Let me get you a menu! Welcome to the Clock Diner in Clock Studios Theme Park! May I ask what I can get you—

t_ch: 钟表披萨蛋糕卷，百层圣代柠檬挞；\n切片蛋糕飞碟堡，苜蓿色拉苏乐达？
t_en: We have Clockie Pizza, Cake Rolls, Hundred-Layered Sundaes, and Lemon Tarts\nSliced Cakes, Floatdisc Burgers, Alfalfa Salad, or SoulGlad?

t_ch: 软糖酸梦彩梦好，圣代百层薯条佳；\n噼咔汽水兑糖浆，脆筒最配爆米花！
t_en: Sour Dreams and Dreamlight are good, sundaes with fries are the best.\nPika soda and syrup or crispy cones and popcorn beat all the rest!

t_ch: 你以为我是真的傻吗？
t_en: Do you really think I'm stupid?

t_ch: 我难道真的不知道什么「只要在匹诺康尼工作满<unbreak>30</unbreak>年，就可以拿到永久居留权」这种瞎话是他们唬我玩的吗？
t_en: You think I don't know that all this "as long as you work in Penacony for <unbreak>30</unbreak> years you can get a permanent residency" stuff is all lies?

t_ch: 没错，我是天天关心家族的人才引进政策，天天算那些乱七八糟的没用积分…但人生总得有点奔头吧？
t_en: That's right, I keep my eye on The Family's talent acquisition policies and count those useless, ridiculous credits every day... But people need ambition, right?

t_ch: 既然不能像他们那样出生在赛道终点，那就怎么也得想办法比身边的人跑得更快点……
t_en: As there's no way for me to be born at the finish line like they were, I just have to find a way to run faster than everyone else around me...

t_ch: 人总不能悲惨到连给自己画饼的资格都没有吧？
t_en: People can't allow their lives to become so tragic that they even lose their right to dream, can they?

t_ch: 知道我最讨厌什么吗？我最讨厌的就是那些自命不凡的本地人！
t_en: Do you know what I hate the most? I hate those arrogant, self-important natives more than anything!

t_ch: 要说这人和人啊，还真就不一样！有的人整天奔波劳碌，才勉强交得起酒店房费；有的人生来屁股下面就摆满了金砖，能在黄金的时刻里肆意挥霍……
t_en: People really just aren't born the same! Some people have to work themselves into the ground all day just to be able to scrape hotel fees, some are born with a silver spoon in their mouths and get to spend all their time whiling their lives away in Golden Hour...

t_ch: 要说勤勤恳恳工作有什么用呢？你再怎么累死累活，能比得了人家含金汤匙出生的大少爷吗？
t_en: What use is there to working hard? It doesn't matter how hard you push yourself, you'll never compare to that rich kid who was born with everything, will you?

t_ch: 我们辛辛苦苦上百个系统时，甚至挣不来人家一顿饭钱，你说可笑不可笑？
t_en: We work ourselves to death for a hundred system hours and can't even buy ourselves a good meal. Don't you think that's ridiculous?

t_ch: 施舍似的带我们去吃喝玩乐，满口什么兄弟情谊…哼，也就那个愣头青看不出来，人那是把咱这群打工仔当宠物遛呢！
t_en: He takes us out to eat, drink, and be merry like it's some kind of charitable act, chatting about "the bonds of brotherhood..." Pfft! Even if that smooth-brained dumbo couldn't tell, he was treating us workers like pets!

t_ch: 最近可真是轻松了不少啊……
t_en: Things have been a lot more relaxed recently...

t_ch: 多亏了傻大个儿，之前那些找茬的、蹭吃蹭喝的，最近可是消停得很啊。
t_en: Thanks to that burly oaf, there's been far fewer people coming in and picking fights for free food and discounts.

t_ch: 哎呦，怎么会有这么缺心眼的家伙啊——随便施舍点小恩小惠就对你感恩戴德，真好打发。
t_en: Ah, how could someone be so slow! Give him a little bit of charity and he's kissing your feet! He's so easy to please.

t_ch: 给几顿剩饭，什么脏活累活都上赶着帮你做了…真是的，每次见了他，我心情都会变好不止一星半点——
t_en: Give him some leftovers and he'll come running to take care of your dirty work and chores... Seriously, every time I see him, I feel a lot better about myself.

t_ch: 什么运动健将，金牌选手…现在还不是混得比我还惨，任我差遣？哈哈哈！
t_en: He was an athlete? A gold-medalist...? Now he's in even more dire straits than I am and taking orders from me? Hahaha!

t_ch: 还想和我聊聊么？就算手边没有特别调制的饮料，随便唠上两句也是好的。
t_en: What do you want to talk about? I'm willing to open up to you, even if I don't have a custom drink in my hand.

t_ch: 想了解关于你的事。
t_en: I want to know more about you.

t_ch: 这个吧台就是最好的聊天场所，你想知道些什么呢？
t_en: This lounge is the perfect place for a chat. What do you want to know?

t_ch: …关于过去。
t_en: ...About your past.

t_ch: 就知道你会问这个。当时情况紧急，没来得及详细说明，我也很少对人谈起。
t_en: I knew that was where you were going. The situation was critical back then, and I didn't have time to explain properly. I don't often talk to people about this, either.

t_ch: …都是很久以前的事了。
t_en: ...It all happened a long time ago.

t_ch: 我的父母是匹诺康尼最常见的那种家族成员，年轻、冲动、自负而野心勃勃，相信梦境里到处都是财富，竭尽所能想爬到更高的位置。
t_en: My parents were the kind of Family members you'd see all the time. Young, impulsive, conceited, and ambitious to a fault. They believed that the Dreamscape was full of wealth and would give anything to claw themselves up to a higher position.

t_ch: 很显然，他们失败了。就在破产前的那天，他们悄悄离开了这颗星球，将刚出生不久的我留在入梦池中。鸢尾花家系的侍者发现了我，并交给了现任家主——梅芙恩小姐。
t_en: Clearly, they failed. Just before the day they went bankrupt, they secretly left the planet and placed me, a newborn baby, in the Dreampool. A servant of the Iris Family found me and handed me over to the current family head - Ms. Maeven.

t_ch: 你有见过他们吗？
t_en: Have you met them before?

t_ch: 没有。这些事都是在我成年后，动用家族关系才调查到的。
t_en: No. I only discovered this once I was older using Family connections.

t_ch: 你会怨恨他们吗？
t_en: Do you hate them?

t_ch: 呵…为什么要怨恨没有任何印象的两个人呢？
t_en: Hmm... Why would I hate two people that I have no recollection of?

t_ch: 刚才和你说的这些事，也都是在我成年后，动用家族关系才调查到的。
t_en: Everything I just told you... I only managed to discover once I was older using Family connections.

t_ch: 他们没有给我留下任何可以追踪的线索，我也没专门去找过他们。
t_en: They didn't leave me any clues to find them, and I have never gone in search of them.

t_ch: 就这样，我加入了鸢尾花家系，往后的经历就是另一个故事了。
t_en: Just like that, I became a part of the Iris Family, what happened after that is a whole other story.

t_ch: …关于鸢尾花家系。
t_en: ...About the Iris Family.

t_ch: 梅芙恩——就是鸢尾花家系的家主，是个沉醉于舞台的人，她相信那种热烈的氛围能消解人与人之间的所有隔阂。在捡到我后，她将我作为未来的演艺明星而着重培养。
t_en: Maeven, the Iris Family Head, is obsessed with the stage. She believes that a passionate atmosphere can break down all barriers between people. So, after she took me in, she focused on raising me as a future acting superstar.

t_ch: 我没有辜负她的期待，在那个领域取得了不错的成绩。艾米绮就是在那时候认识的，她是我的后辈，同样是备受期待的孩子，在演出方面拥有非凡的天赋。
t_en: I did not disappoint her and managed to achieve a lot of success in this field. I met Amiky back then, too. She was my junior and also a child who shouldered a lot of expectations due to her outstanding acting talents.

t_ch: 但很快我就厌倦了这种生活。鲜花和掌声只会给我带来深深的疲惫感，舞台上的生活太过虚幻，那不是真正的我。
t_en: However, I quickly grew tired of this life. The flowers and applause only gave me a deep sense of fatigue. Living a life on the stage is all too illusory, and that's not the real me.

t_ch: …或许，我是在寻找着什么。
t_en: ...Perhaps, I was searching for something.

t_ch: 那你找到了吗？
t_en: Did you find it?

t_ch: 可能吧，我也说不清楚。谁知道呢？但我喜欢现在的生活。
t_en: Maybe, I'm not even sure myself. Who knows? But I like the life I have now.

t_ch: 我们每个人都在寻找着什么。
t_en: We are all searching for something.

t_ch: 我们也都同时失去了什么——哈哈哈，这种句子常挂在嘴边可没什么意思。
t_en: Then we are all losing something else at the same time. Hahaha, these quips are no fun to be constantly mentioning.

t_ch: 后来，在和梅芙恩大吵一架后，我离开了鸢尾花家系，独自开了这间酒吧。
t_en: After that, I had a huge argument with Maeven and left the Iris Family. Then, I came and opened this lounge by myself.

t_ch: 再往后的事，你也清楚了。作为调饮师的生活很单调，但也很丰富，值得反复回味。
t_en: And you know the rest from there. Life as a drinksmith is monotonous but rich. It is worth savoring.

t_ch: …关于你和艾米绮。
t_en: ...About you and Amiky.

t_ch: 我没有直接说出来，就是担心会影响怪物们的情绪。
t_en: I never said it outright because I was worried about upsetting the monsters.

t_ch: 但没想到，猎犬家系来得比预想得要快，更没料到的是，你们会去「黄金的时刻」找我，还恰好听到了我和艾米绮的对话。生活真是充满了意外。
t_en: But, I never expected that the Bloodhound Family would have arrived so quickly. Even more unexpected was the fact that you happened to overhear me and Amiky talking when you came to Golden Hour to search for me. Life really is full of surprises, isn't it?

t_ch: 你打算回到鸢尾花家系吗？
t_en: Do you plan on returning to the Iris Family?

t_ch: 不打算，但艾米绮说得没错，仅凭理想没法保住这家酒吧。要是没有我和梅芙恩的这层关系，恐怕怪物们早就被清理掉了。
t_en: I don't. But Amiky was right, I cannot save this lounge with ideals alone. If it weren't for my relationship with Maeven, I fear that the monsters would have all been cleared out long ago.

t_ch: 和家族周旋是个麻烦事，后面还得再想想办法。
t_en: Dealing with The Family is always troublesome, I'll need to think more about this in the future.

t_ch: 艾米绮那边要怎么交代呢？
t_en: How do you deal with Amiky?

t_ch: 别看艾米绮蛮横不讲理的样子，其实心软得很，稍微哄几句就消气了。
t_en: Amiky isn't the rude and unreasonable girl that you think. She's actually very gentle, she just needs a few words of comfort and she calms down.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>调饮…就是对话的过程。</color>我现在算是越来越体会到这点了。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Mixing drinks... is like the art of conversation.</color> I feel like I'm coming to understand this more and more.

t_ch: 沟通是信任的基础，要是将话藏在心里不说，被误会了也没法怪别人，对吧？
t_en: Communication is the foundation of trust. If you keep things in your heart and don't say them out loud, then you cannot blame other people when they misunderstand you, can you?

t_ch: …关于未来。
t_en: ...About the future.

t_ch: 多亏你的帮助，我们暂时保住了「惊梦酒吧」，但这样终究不是长久之计。
t_en: Thanks to you, we managed to hold onto the Dreamjolt Hostelry, but this is not a long-term fix.

t_ch: 在未来…我希望能让更多人接纳它们，让怪物们能在外面的社会里生活。梦境酒店实在太过狭隘，它们都很想见识更大的世界。
t_en: In the future... I hope more people will learn to accept monsters and allow them to enter society. The hotel in the dreamscape is just too small, they long to see the big, wide world.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，现在说这些还是有点太理想了。慢慢来，走一步看一步吧。
t_en: Haha, maybe it's a little too idealistic to be saying things like that for now. We'll take it slowly, step by step.

t_ch: 反正，我们的时间还很长。
t_en: We have plenty of time ahead of us, after all.

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 只是和你打个招呼。
t_en: I'm just stopping by to say hi.

t_ch: 没事情的话，就在酒吧里随便逛逛呗。记得多跟怪物们聊聊，它们都可想你了。
t_en: If you're not busy, why not take a stroll around the lounge? Make sure you talk with the monsters, too! They've missed you so much!

t_ch: 造型古典的艺术钟，出现在餐桌上也毫无违和感。
t_en: The classically-designed art clock looks completely out of place on the dinner table.

t_ch: 这块钟表摆放得十分端正，似乎承载着某种仪式性作用。
t_en: This clock is positioned extremely precisely and seems to serve some kind of ritualistic function.

t_ch: 不要试图用手去碰它，这玩意看起来就很贵。
t_en: Don't touch it. Just one look at it tells you that this is expensive.

t_ch: 时钟的指针丝毫不动。可能这就是「愿时光永驻此刻」的真正意涵。
t_en: The clock's hands are motionless. Perhaps this is the true meaning of "may this moment last forever."

t_ch: 这座雕像似乎被某种巨大的外力推倒在地，但依旧完好无损。
t_en: This statue seems to have been tipped over by some great force, but it remains undamaged.

t_ch: 象征家族权威的雕像，不知因何被推倒、拦住了你前进的路。
t_en: A statue that symbolizes the great authority of The Family has been knocked over, blocking your path ahead.

t_ch: 你不知道这是怎么回事，反正不是你干的。
t_en: You don't know what this is about, but you know it wasn't you that did it.

t_ch: 在常人看来这是一组平平无奇的隐夜鸫雕像，但他们其实各有所指。
t_en: To a regular person, they seem to be nothing more than a group of unremarkable Nightingale Statues, but they each have their own meaning.

t_ch: 这尊雕像象征着里奥撒缪斯，橡木家系的第一位家主。传说他在一位忆者的指引下拔出了紧紧嵌在梦泡中的神剑，深孚众望，橡木家系也由此成为匹诺康尼的核心势力。
t_en: This statue symbolizes Riothamus, first Family Head of the Oak Family. Legend says that under the guidance of a Memokeeper, he was able to remove the divine sword that was stuck fast inside a dream bubble. This won him great renown with the people and allowed the Oak Family to become a central power in Penacony.

t_ch: 在常人看来这是一组平平无奇的隐夜鸫雕像，但他们其实各有所指。
t_en: To a regular person, they seem to be nothing more than a group of unremarkable Nightingale Statues, but they each have their own meaning.

t_ch: 这尊雕像象征着贝尔蒂埃，猎犬家系的第一位家主。传说他是里奥撒缪斯的左膀右臂，忠贞不二，枪术高超。当橡木家主遭到背叛濒死时，是他陪伴这位家主到最后一刻。
t_en: This statue symbolizes Bediver, first Family Head of the Bloodhound Family. Legend states that he was Riothamus' right-hand man - faithful to the end and skilled in battle. When the Oak Family Head was betrayed and left to die, it was he that accompanied him in his final moments.

t_ch: 在常人看来这是一组平平无奇的隐夜鸫雕像，但他们其实各有所指。
t_en: To a regular person, they seem to be nothing more than a group of unremarkable Nightingale Statues, but they each have their own meaning.

t_ch: 这尊雕像象征着坦特里斯，鸢尾花家系的第一位家主。传说他的武艺是六大家系中最顶尖的，却也多愁善感。他与公司员工坠入爱河，而这段恋情最终招来了血腥的内战。
t_en: This statue symbolizes Tantris, first Family Head of the Iris Family. Legend states that though his martial prowess exceeded that of all of the other Families, he was a kind and gentle man. He fell deeply in love with an IPC employee. This romance eventually led to a bloody civil war.

t_ch: 在常人看来这是一组平平无奇的隐夜鸫雕像，但他们其实各有所指。
t_en: To a regular person, they seem to be nothing more than a group of unremarkable Nightingale Statues, but they each have their own meaning.

t_ch: 这尊雕像象征着果戈尔凡，苜蓿草家系的第一位家主。有关他的记载不多，人们只知道他在对公司的战争中是一名变节的狱卒，始终掌管着公司在匹诺康尼的金库。
t_en: This statue symbolizes Gogrefan, first Family Head of the Alfalfa Family. Little is known about him, except that he was a prison guard that defected during the war against the IPC and that he retained control of the IPC treasury in Penacony.

t_ch: 在常人看来这是一组平平无奇的隐夜鸫雕像，但他们其实各有所指。
t_en: To a regular person, they seem to be nothing more than a group of unremarkable Nightingale Statues, but they each have their own meaning.

t_ch: 这尊雕像象征着凯尔康尼，隐夜鸫家系的第一位家主。他主持了对边陲监狱的生活化改造，也是梦境拓荒运动的主负责人。他缔造了现今意义上的「梦想之地」匹诺康尼。
t_en: This statue symbolizes Celcony, first Family Head of the Nightingale Family. He took charge of the enlivening transformation of the frontier prisons and was the main person in charge of the Dreamscape pioneering movement. He created the "land of the dreams," Penacony, as it exists today.

t_ch: 在常人看来这是一组平平无奇的隐夜鸫雕像，但他们其实各有所指。
t_en: To a regular person, they seem to be nothing more than a group of unremarkable Nightingale Statues, but they each have their own meaning.

t_ch: 这尊雕像象征着一位神秘人物——潜伏于五大家系背后的影子家系家主——卡拉赫爵士。他是一位「神秘」的命途行者，暗中控制着五大家系和匹诺康尼大小事务。
t_en: This statue symbolizes a mysterious figure - Head of the Shadow Family that lurks behind the Five Lineages - Sir Callagh. He is a Pathstrider of Enigmata, controlling the Five Lineages from the shadows, along with all political affairs of Penacony.

t_ch: 有关六尊隐夜鸫雕像的故事就到此为止了。看得开心吗？如果开心，那么恭喜你——
t_en: We shall leave the story of the Nightingale Statue for now. Did you enjoy those stories? If you did, then congratulations...

t_ch: 刚才你所看到的一切都是瞎掰的，或者说，虚构的历史。匹诺康尼历史上最多曾有过七大家系，由于种种变故形成了如今的五大家系，但根本不存在所谓的「影子家系」。
t_en: Everything you read just now was nonsense. Or, should I say, a fabricated history. In Penacony's history, the maximum number of Families that have existed is seven, and various changes over time formed the five lineages that we have today. There is no such thing as a "Shadow Family."

t_ch: 各大家系的首位家主也全都另有其人，且大多是些名不见经传的小角色，例如：橡木家系的马格诺只是个阴险狡诈的政治犯，鸢尾花的阿斯娜则是蒙冤入狱的宇宙歌者……
t_en: The first Family Heads of each lineage were also different people, the majority of them being unknown characters that feature in no such legends. For example: The Head of the Oak Family, Magnoy, was nothing more than a corrupt politician. Asna of the Iris Family was a universal singer who was wrongly imprisoned...

t_ch: 苜蓿草的拉契卡是公司传统项目部的贪污犯，隐夜鸫的图尔度是个让公司蒙受巨额损失的疯人学者——你觉得不够史诗？那就对了，因为历史本就是由无数小人物编织而成的。
t_en: Lakkika of the Alfalfa Family was an embezzler working in the IPC Traditional Project Department and Tordu of the Nightingale Family was an insane scholar that caused the IPC crushing financial losses... Are those stories not epic enough for you? Good, because history is simply created through the actions of countless, unremarkable people.

t_ch: 还有，一个忠告：耳听为虚，眼见为实。祝你「开拓」好运！
t_en: One more word of advice: That which you hear is false, only seeing is believing. Good luck on your "trailblazing!"

t_ch: 一尊平平无奇的隐夜鸫雕像，并不承载任何明显或隐含的寓意及宣示意图。
t_en: An unremarkable Nightingale Statue that illustrates no obvious or hidden propaganda or intentions.

t_ch: 为什么橡木家系家主要在公馆玄关摆这么多隐夜鸫雕像？真是令人费解。
t_en: Why would the Oak Family Head have so many Nightingale Statues in the foyer? It really is incomprehensible.

t_ch: 隐夜鸫雕像很想注视你，但它没有眼睛。
t_en: The Nightingale Statue wishes to stare at you, but it has no eyes.

t_ch: 道理你都明白，但隐夜鸫为什么这么大？
t_en: You understand the logic, but why are the Nightingales so large?

t_ch: 一幅挂在墙上的装饰性绘画，其中的形象酷似折纸小鸟——不过是偏写实风格的。
t_en: A decorative painting is hung on the wall, the image on it is a precise rendering of an origami bird... But in a realistic style.

t_ch: 画上的折纸鸟与你见过的小鸟们不尽相同，或许这反映了家族内部艺术风格的变迁。
t_en: The origami bird in the picture is unlike those that you have met before, perhaps reflecting the changing of artistic styles within The Family.

t_ch: 桌上翠鸟身形微晃，仰头发出声清脆的啼鸣；画中红鸟则眉目低垂，音调绵长戚悲——
t_en: The green bird on the table sways slightly as it raises its head to let out a crisp, clear cry. The red bird in the painting has its brow furrowed as it sings in a low tone of sadness...

t_ch: 「她开口了！啊！请继续说下去吧，我画中的爱侣！」
t_en: "She is ready to speak! Ah! Please, continue, my lover of painting-made!"

t_ch: 「不在这里、也不在那里！你为何不在我所及之处！」
t_en: "You're not here, you're not there! Why can you not be by my side!"

t_ch: 一座神秘的水池？主持修建这座公馆的人究竟在暗示什么？
t_en: A mysterious pond? Just what was the person that led the construction of this pavilion hinting at?

t_ch: 水池里的液体令你联想到匹诺康尼特产苏乐达——令人惊讶的是，它确实是苏乐达。但为什么？
t_en: The liquid in the pool makes you think of the Penacony specialty, SoulGlad. Shockingly, it actually is SoulGlad! But why?

t_ch: 生活小窍门：水池里的水并不会主动攻击人类。放心洗手吧。
t_en: Life tip: Water in a pond will never actively attack humans. You can wash your hands with peace of mind.

t_ch: 一座巨大的沙盘，以固定比例真实还原了「黄金的时刻」街巷风貌。
t_en: A giant sandpit that has realistically replicated the streets and alleys of "Golden Hour" on a smaller scale.

t_ch: 据说沙盘内部设有隐秘的机关，指向公馆更深处的秘密。
t_en: Rumor has it that there is a secret mechanism inside the sandpit that leads to secrets that lay further in the depths of the pavilion.

t_ch: 各大家系家主会定期在此处展开会谈，商议梦境都市的日常事务、经营情况，以及未来的发展规划。这座沙盘就是他们流程的一环。
t_en: Each Family Head will periodically hold a meeting here to discuss the daily affairs, operational status and future development plans of the Dreamscape here. This sandpit is used as part of this process.

t_ch: 公馆的共享书架。家族成员们会捐赠出自己的私人藏书，供众人自由翻阅。
t_en: The pavilion communal bookshelf. Family members will donate books from their own private collections for others to read freely.

t_ch: 对家族成员来说，书本和知识都是可以共享的。
t_en: As far as members of The Family are concerned, books and knowledge are things that can be shared.

t_ch: 这面书架上陈列着家族的关键资料。正当你试图拿起一本看看时，侍者的声音刺入你的脑内，立刻制止了你。
t_en: The shelf is stacked with crucial information regarding The Family. Just as you reach out and attempt to pick one up, a servant's voice pierces through your mind and stops you in your tracks.

t_ch: 家族的机密书架，其中记载了一些不为人知的内事，外人无权翻阅——包括你。
t_en: The Family's secret bookshelf records certain affairs that are not to be divulged to outsiders, outsiders are not permitted to read these materials... This includes you.

t_ch: …说好的「书本和知识都是可以共享的」呢？
t_en: ...I thought "books and knowledge were things that can be shared?"

t_ch: 象征猎犬家系的徽记，被绫罗绸缎包围的猎犬仍旧忠心不移。
t_en: A crest that symbolizes the Bloodhound Family. Wrapped in silks and satin, the loyal hound remains faithful still.

t_ch: 象征鸢尾花家系的徽记，优雅绽放的花朵散发着动人心魄的魅力。
t_en: A crest that symbolizes the Iris Family. A flower blooms elegantly, exuding a charm that captivates the hearts of man.

t_ch: 象征橡木家系的徽记，一圈圈年轮如靶心般指引众人前进的方向。
t_en: A crest that symbolizes the Oak Family. It shows growth rings of a great tree guiding everyone towards progress, like the bullseye on a target.

t_ch: 象征苜蓿草家系的徽记，向心聚拢的叶片昭示着无与伦比的凝聚力。
t_en: A crest that symbolizes the Alfalfa Family. Leaves gathering together towards the center display an unparalleled cohesive force.

t_ch: 象征隐夜鸫家系的徽记，枝头的鸟儿亦是衔枝的筑梦师。
t_en: A crest that symbolizes the Nightingale Family. The bird sitting on the branch is also a Dreamweaver, holding the branch in place.

t_ch: 知更鸟的宣传海报，出于某种纪念意义而被收藏于此。
t_en: Robin's promotional posters. They've been collected here as some sort of keepsake.

t_ch: 当你靠近时，一种天启般的预感倾泻着灌进你的脑海，清晰又强烈——
t_en: As you approach, a premonition rushes over your mind like a revelation, clear and powerful...

t_ch: 那二人终将如化冻河冰，冰下裂隙滋生，河上湍流暗涌。
t_en: The fate of those two is like that of ice on a frozen river. As the ice cracks, the river will grow turbulent.

t_ch: 同谐的梦中，殊途者异梦。
t_en: In a dream of the Harmony, those of different paths follow different dreams.

t_ch: 知更鸟演出时的宣传海报，出于某种纪念意义而被收藏于此。
t_en: Robin's promotional posters. They've been collected here as some sort of keepsake.

t_ch: 她是如此的美丽……
t_en: She is so beautiful...

t_ch: 「假如我说，你真美啊，请稍稍停留——」
t_en: "What if I said... Stay, you're so beautiful!"

t_ch: 公馆主人的众多收藏品之一，彼此间几乎没有共同点。他的个人美学简直像家族的成分一样复杂。
t_en: One of the pavilion master's many collected items, none of which seem to share anything in common. His personal tastes seem to be as complicated as the composition of The Family itself.

t_ch: 你分不清这些收藏品比起外面的同款究竟有什么特别。不过有没有可能——它们本就无关紧要呢？
t_en: You cannot tell just what is special about these pieces compared to the identical models outside. Although perhaps... They're not important at all?

t_ch: 你发现自己并不太能欣赏房间主人的高雅品味。
t_en: You realize that you are just not able to appreciate the acquired taste of the chamber's master.

t_ch: 巨大的同心圆雕像，据说可以为祈祷者带来精神力量。
t_en: A giant statue of concentric shapes, it is said to be able to bring vigor and vitality to those who pray to it.

t_ch: 这念头或许有些不合时宜，但…祈祷真的有用吗？
t_en: This thought may not be entirely appropriate but... Does prayer even work?

t_ch: 透过圆心，你仿佛能感受到希佩的笑意。
t_en: You feel as if you can feel Xipe smiling at you through the center of the circles.

t_ch: 据说有些侍者会在无人注意的角落将雕像当做标靶——不知道那些手感火热的投手们还能不能享受来自家族的包容。
t_en: Rumor has it that some servants use the statues as targets when they are out of sight... You wonder if those hot-handed pitchers will ever receive forgiveness from The Family?

t_ch: 你曾经听那位仙舟艺人女孩说过，以前他们家里吃饭也用这么大的圆桌。
t_en: You once heard that young artist on the Xianzhou say that her family used to eat at a giant round table of this size.

t_ch: 你听说公馆的主人有非常严重的强迫症，但这张圆桌的状态证明他痊愈了。
t_en: It's said that the master of this pavilion suffers from severe compulsions, but this table clearly shows that he has been cured.

t_ch: 圆桌不似长桌，没有主次和对立关系，极有可能是「同谐」命途的星神候补。
t_en: Unlike a long table, round tables have no sense of priority or opposition, it is very likely that they are an Aeonic candidate for the Path of Harmony.

t_ch: 圆桌不似长桌，没有主次和对立关系，「均衡」的星神见了它高低也得鞠两个躬。
t_en: Unlike a long table, round tables have no sense of priority or opposition. The Aeon of Equilibrium would still have to bow twice upon seeing it.

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎！呆站在这干嘛，别挡着我上菜呢。刚做好的美味的披萨，嘿，松脆，热腾得很！
t_en: Mwahaha! What are you staring at me for? You're in the way of my food! Freshly made pizza is so crispy and hot!

t_ch: 悄咪咪吃掉一块，应该不会有人发现吧咔咔咔！
t_en: No one will notice if I sneak a little slice, will they? Gahaha!

t_ch: 汪！这是客人点的单，不可以偷吃哇！
t_en: Woof! A customer ordered this, you can't steal it!

t_ch: 缺角的披萨，看起来太明显了哇。
t_en: A slice of pizza with a corner missing is so obvious!

t_ch: 怕什么！嘻嘻，你再吃掉一块不就看不出来了嘛。大份的能吃，小份的也能吃，没毛病！
t_en: What are you worried about! Hehe, if you take another bite, then you can't even tell! If it's a big portion, you can eat it... Same with a small one, no problem!

t_ch: 需要我去告诉舒翁吗？
t_en: Do I need to tell Siobhan about this?

t_ch: 呜呜…对不起，我不该动这种歪心思，我会做个乖孩子的，真的，要不…信我一次？
t_en: Waaa... I'm sorry! I shouldn't think such wicked thoughts! I'll be good, I promise! How about... you trust me just this once?

t_ch: …我可以也吃一块吗？
t_en: ...Can I have a piece, too?

t_ch: 嘻嘻，请…请。吃得越多，暴露的概率越小！大的变小，正正好好！
t_en: Hehe, please, be my guest! The more we eat, the less likely it is they'll find out! Big slices get smaller, it's perfect!

t_ch: 汪！危险！这个披萨就交给我去送哇，你们都不准靠近！
t_en: Woof! Danger! Leave the pizza delivery to me, and none of you are allowed to get close!

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎嘎嘎！哪位客人要的披萨，介意我偷偷尝尝嘛，就…就一口！
t_en: Waaahahaha! You won't even let me try the pizza they ordered? Just a little... just... just one bite!

t_ch: 汪！饮料放在您桌上了！披萨跟我完全没关系哇！
t_en: Woof! The drinks are on your table! That pizza has nothing to do with me!

t_ch: …您是想和我聊聊？当然可以，这儿的氛围很悠闲，正适合说说心里话。
t_en: ...You want to talk with me? Of course, the atmosphere is very relaxed, it's perfect for talking about what's on your mind.

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)

t_ch: 既然是您主动发起的邀约，还是请先开口吧？
t_en: As you invited me to talk, you should probably start?

t_ch: 想听听你对这家酒吧的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about this lounge.

t_ch: 我很喜欢，恐怕在整个梦境里，都找不到如此…有人情味的地方了。
t_en: I like it a lot! I fear there is nowhere else in the Dreamscape that you can find a place with such a... human touch.

t_ch: 虽然酒吧里生活的都是怪物，可它们远比那些宾客要更加亲切和友善，也比宣扬谐乐的家族要更温暖。
t_en: It may just be monsters that live here, but they're a lot kinder and friendly than those visitors. They're a lot warmer than that harmony-touting family, too!

t_ch: 要想找个真正能让人放松的地方，这里再合适不过了。
t_en: If you want somewhere that can really help you relax, you won't find better than here.

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.

t_ch: …您？向搭讪对象询问这种直接的话题，您还真是不懂得循序渐进的道理呢。
t_en: ...You? That's a real direct way of speaking to someone you're chatting with. You really don't seem to grasp the concept of taking it slow.

t_ch: 依我在酒吧的见闻来看，这里的主人，还有那些怪物们都很信任您，说明您至少是个善良的人，没有将它们视作肆意差遣的对象。
t_en: From what I have learned in this lounge, both the owner and the monsters seem to trust you a lot. This means that you are at least a kind person, you don't just see them as things you can order around as you please.

t_ch: 能做到这点，就已经比沉陷在梦境中、只知道享乐的人们要好太多了。我认为您相当优秀——不是恭维，是真心这么觉得哦。
t_en: Your empathy already makes you far better than the people drowning in this Dreamscape and only seeking to indulge in fun. I think you are quite outstanding — It's not flattery. I really do this so.

t_ch: 好有趣的狗狗啾！
t_en: What a fun dog, tweet!

t_ch: 人家也想喝饮料啾！
t_en: I want a drink, too, tweet!

t_ch: 我们是偷偷跑出来的。
t_en: We secretly snuck out here.

t_ch: 不要把我们赶回去啾！
t_en: Please don't make us go back, tweet!

t_ch: 抓到我们也没奖励啾！
t_en: You won't get a reward for catching us, tweet!

t_ch: 嘿，我看你们很有讲脱口秀的天分，跟我去参加「奇异果」电台怎么样？
t_en: Hm, I think you two would be great at stand-up comedy! Why don't you join me at Kiwis radio station?

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)

t_ch: …嗯？你找我有事吗？
t_en: Oh? Can I help you with anything?

t_ch: 想听听你的脱口秀表演。
t_en: I want to hear your stand-up comedy performance.

t_ch: 皮皮西笑话讲腻了，换成狗狗笑话也不错！我现场编一个啊。别急，在想了。
t_en: I'm bored with telling Pepeshi jokes, I am going to start telling dog jokes! I'll come up with one now... Just a second, I'm thinking.

t_ch: 想到了！听我说——有只狗狗啊，最近换了新主人，它问：「主人，为什么你从来都不肯亲近我」，主人回答：「我始终都把你当成最好的伙伴啊」。
t_en: I know! Okay... There's this dog, and he recently got a new master. He asked his master: "Hey master, why do you never treat me like you love me?" The master replied: "I've always treated you like my best friend!"

t_ch: 狗狗又问了：「那为什么你总是居高临下地对我呢」…你知道是为什么吗？
t_en: Then the dog asked again: "Then why do you always look down on me..." Do you know why?

t_ch: …因为主人不爱它？
t_en: ...Because the master doesn't love him?

t_ch: …因为狗狗很自卑？
t_en: ...Because the dog has low self-esteem?

t_ch: 因为狗狗原本的主人是个皮皮西人——哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Because the dog's old master was a Pepeshi... Hahaha!

t_ch: 哎，现编的笑话果然不够味。要不这样，干脆聘请我来你们酒吧做驻场脱口秀演员吧！
t_en: Ah, jokes are never funny when you make them up on the spot... I know, why don't you hire me as a resident stand-up comedian in your lounge?

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.

t_ch: 嗨，这位观众真是问了个好问题！这里是酒吧，讲出来的话当然要符合氛围才行。
t_en: Hey, a member of the audience has asked a fine question! As this is a lounge, your words have to fit in with the atmosphere.

t_ch: 所以我会回答：「匹诺康尼有那么多家酒吧，酒吧里有那么多客人，可你在搭讪时偏偏选中了我」…怎么样！够深情，够动人心弦吧？！
t_en: So, I would respond: "There are so many lounges in Penacony, with so many guests in each lounge, yet you chose to talk with me..." Well? What do you think? That would blow you away, right?

t_ch: …你接的太烂了。
t_en: ...You're terrible at this.

t_ch: 哎，我也知道，我就是个逗观众开心的，煽情这套玩不太来。等我学成了再给你表演，等着啊！
t_en: Ah, I know... I just like to make audiences happy, I'm no good at playing with emotion and stuff like that... Let me learn and I'll put on a proper show for you, you just wait!

t_ch: 我喜欢你的回答！
t_en: That's a great response!

t_ch: …不是吧，这么老套的梗都有人喜欢？莫非是我的表演技巧太好了？
t_en: No way, there's someone who likes even an cliché line like that one? Or could it be that my acting skills are just too amazing?

t_ch: 我想想…嗯，要不干脆转行去演话剧好了，老是讲脱口秀确实没啥前途啊。
t_en: Let me think... Hmm, maybe I should change careers and start acting? There really isn't much future in being a stand-up comedy host, after all...

t_ch: 嗨！点个头，哈个腰，与客人共度的夜晚真美妙！欢迎光临「惊梦酒吧」！
t_en: Hey! Nod your head and give a bow, a night with customers makes me feel wow! Welcome to the Dreamjolt Hostelry!

t_ch: 原来是您。调饮辛苦了！按照您的意思，我正站在酒吧入口处看门呢。
t_en: Oh, it's you! Thanks for your hard work mixing drinks! As you suggested, I'm standing by the door and keeping an eye out.

t_ch: 为什么不是「司令」在这？
t_en: Why isn't Mr. Bigwig at the door?

t_ch: 哎！它就是个苏乐达的派送员，懂什么看门。不像我——嗓音洪亮，高大威猛，往这一站，特别有范！要是再穿个黑西装，就更有排场了！
t_en: Hah! They're just the SoulGlad deliveryman, what would they know about being a door attendant? They're not like me... They don't have a loud and clear voice. They aren't tall and mighty, capable of leaving a lasting impression! If I only had a tuxedo on, I'd be perfect!

t_ch: 我好像没安排过这件事吧？
t_en: I don't remember planning this?

t_ch: 怪我！怪我！没及时向您报告。我寻思着酒吧的门常敞开，要是顾客到来却没人招待，那怎么能行！所以就擅自行动了。
t_en: My bad! My bad! I didn't report back to you earlier. I was thinking about the fact that the lounge door is always open, it's no good if guests arrive and there is no one there to greet them! So, I took action.

t_ch: 您放心！有我在，这家酒吧绝对不会出事！就算出事，也是小事！
t_en: Rest assured! There will be no issues in this lounge! If there are, they will only be small ones!

t_ch: 嗨！脚并拢，头埋低，时时倾听客人的心意！欢迎光临「惊梦酒吧」！
t_en: Hey! Feet together, head down, our customers always wear the crown! Welcome to the Dreamjolt Hostelry!

t_ch: 你也是门卫？啧，那么高的个子，看起来威风，说不定脑子里早就全生锈了。
t_en: You're a door attendant, too? Tsk, look at how tall you are, standing all high and mighty. Has your brain gone rusty from being so high in the sky?

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)

t_ch: 哼。看在你没有站着跟我说话的份上，就勉为其难地聊聊吧。
t_en: Humph. As you're not standing over me while we talk, I guess we can make conversation.

t_ch: 想听听你对这家酒吧的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about this lounge.

t_ch: 挺好的地方，不吵闹，没有那些凭身高显摆的傻蛋。喏，这个大块头不会去欺负那两个小型怪物，它们的关系好像还不错，这就很难得了。
t_en: I like this place. It is not too loud and there aren't too many of those tall idiots bragging about their height. Tsk, this big brute won't bully those two little monsters. They look like they get on well — This is a rare sight.

t_ch: 我回去问问皮皮西俱乐部的管理员，看他们想不想来这团建。找个能让所有会员都满意的地方挺不容易的，他们不喜欢外人多的场所。
t_en: I'll go and ask the Pepeshi Club administrator if they want to come here for a team-building event. It's not easy to find a place that everyone is happy with. They don't like places with lots of outsiders.

t_ch: 有消息了再通知你…还是那个酒保？算了，就你吧，至少你不会给我太多压力。
t_en: Shall I let you know when I hear something... Or the drinksmith? Forget it, I'll talk to you, I know you won't pressure me too much.

t_ch: …这是在说我矮吗？
t_en: ...Are you calling me short?

t_ch: 原因出在你身上吧？
t_en: That's more of an issue on your end, isn't it?

t_ch: 少废话。拒绝和比我高的人讲道理，问就是我对！
t_en: Quit your nonsense. I will not try and reason with people taller than me. I am always right!

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.

t_ch: 呃…这个问题有点突然，我得缓缓。
t_en: Uhh... That question came out of nowhere, let me think.

t_ch: 咳咳。嗯，不好意思，但我必须拒绝你。你是个远高于皮皮西标准线的人类，而我是个恪尽职守的门卫，我们之间是根本不可能的。除非你……
t_en: Ahem. Umm, I'm sorry, but I must turn you down. You are a human, far taller than the height standards of the Pepeshi people, and I am a dutiful door attendant, it would never work between us. Unless...

t_ch: 没什么除非！哼，就是这样！我们连朋友都做不了！
t_en: No, unless nothing! Humph, I said what I said! We can't even be friends!

t_ch: 钢琴家安静坐在丝绒织成的软垫上，神情似水般平和，望向远处的目光微微含笑。
t_en: Serene, the pianist sits on a velvet cushion, his countenance remaining tranquil as still water. A faint smile adorns his face as he gazes into the distance.

t_ch: 究竟是这嬉闹的光景，让他回想起了过往和朋友度过的欢乐时光，还是单纯喜爱这些有趣的怪物，任由自己沉醉在酒吧惬意的氛围中？
t_en: Perhaps the whimsical scene evokes his memories of joyous moments shared with friends? Or perhaps he simply harbors a deep fondness for these amusing monsters, immersing himself in the cozy ambiance of the lounge?

t_ch: 只有音乐知道。
t_en: Only music knows the answer.

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)

t_ch: 钢琴家做出「请」的手势，并未有过多言语。
t_en: Without speaking much, the pianist gives you a gentle gesture.

t_ch: 想听听你对这家酒吧的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about this lounge.

t_ch: 柔和的话语随乐声而至，不知何时，那架钢琴出现在了麦克斯的身边。
t_en: His soft words are accompanied by a melody. Before you realize it, the piano materializes beside Max.

t_ch: 我们很喜欢这里——我的小先生，他很久没有这样开心过了。
t_en: We love this place. My dear master hasn't been so happy in quite some time.

t_ch: 梦境给了我们生命，却没有赋予我们对等的地位和自由。人们都将我们视作纯粹的自动演奏工具，没有灵魂，坏了就换，不值得投入任何感情。
t_en: While the Dreamscape has granted us life, it hasn't granted us any social status or freedom. People view us as mere automated machines, devoid of souls, conveniently replaceable when damaged, and unworthy of emotional attachment.

t_ch: 冷漠不会带来任何好的结果，只会加深彼此的孤独和痛苦。
t_en: Apathy will not bear favorable outcomes, only deepening our loneliness and pain.

t_ch: 因此，我很欣赏这位酒吧主人的做法。尊重与陪伴，永远是表达爱意最好的方式。
t_en: Therefore, I appreciate the lounge owner's approach. Respect and companionship are always the best way of expressing love.

t_ch: 想听听你对怪物们的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about monsters.

t_ch: 低沉的嗓音混杂着急促鼓点，在你的耳畔骤然响起。
t_en: A low voice, amidst the sharp beat of a drum, suddenly resonates in your ears.

t_ch: 都是群傻了吧唧的家伙，过去被欺负成那样还不反抗，没骨气！
t_en: Those imbeciles! They're spineless worms who don't even have the guts to fight back against the mistreatment they endure!

t_ch: 人类根本不值得相信，非得等到被抛弃了才明白——当然，麦克斯除外。
t_en: Humans don't deserve any trust — except for Max, of course. But those fools just remain blind to this fact until the very day they are abandoned.

t_ch: 不过看它们现在过得还挺开心，希望这里的生活能长久持续下去吧。
t_en: But now they seem to be happy, so let's hope the life here will last long.

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.

t_ch: 恍惚间，你听见了某位绅士的声音，语气克制礼貌，如弦乐般舒缓而悠扬。
t_en: Out of nowhere, you hear the voice of a gentleman. His tone is restrained and polite, soothing and melodious like string music.

t_ch: 您是个很善良的人，就像麦克斯先生那样，我从您的谈吐间看得出来。
t_en: You possess a benevolent soul, just as Maestro Max does. I can discern it from the way you speak.

t_ch: 但麦克斯先生已经有了值得信任的伙伴，我们约好了，要在梦境里演奏一辈子音乐。
t_en: However, Maestro Max already has trusted companions, as we have promised to create music within the Dreamscape for the remainder of our existence.

t_ch: 如果您接近麦克斯先生，只是出于同情或是好奇的话，我希望您最好收起这份情感，这对一位纯粹的音乐家而言无异于冒犯。
t_en: If you approach Maestro Max out of mere sympathy or curiosity, I implore you to set aside such sentiments, as they would be perceived as offensive to a pure musician.

t_ch: 用于服务的自动道具…它们真的拥有灵魂，能够感受快乐，欣赏艺术吗？
t_en: Automated machines for service... Are they truly capable of possessing souls, experiencing pleasure, and appreciating art?

t_ch: 或许我该好好在这观察段时间，反思自己的观点了。
t_en: Perhaps I should reflect upon myself during my stay here.

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)

t_ch: 行，这里的音乐也正适合畅谈，你想聊什么话题？
t_en: Alright, the music here is perfect for a conversation. What would you like to discuss?

t_ch: 想听听你对怪物们的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about monsters.

t_ch: 我来到匹诺康尼前就听说过，在梦境里有种供人娱乐的自动道具，很听话，完全按照命令行事。但工具就是工具，替代不了人类。
t_en: Before my arrival in Penacony, I heard that there are automated machines designed for amusement in the Dreamscape. They're obedient and always do their job well. However, tools are tools, and they'll never replace human beings.

t_ch: 别的都无所谓，但我无论如何都没法忍受那些自动乐团。它们演奏的不是音乐，是毫无灵魂的噪音，简直亵渎了那些旋律！
t_en: I'm fine with most things here, but those automated orchestras... What they produce is not music, but soulless cacophony!

t_ch: 至于酒吧里的怪物，嗯…我承认，它们让我看到了自动道具的另一面。从某种角度来说，它们确实是「生命」，对世界有着和我们类似的体验。
t_en: As for the monsters in the lounge, well... I admit they've presented me with a new perspective on the automated machines. In a way, they can be considered "beings" with a similar perception of the world as we do.

t_ch: …那自动乐团呢？
t_en: ...So, how about the automated orchestra?

t_ch: 你还真是会挑问题。对，我得好好想想。但在得出结论前，我对它们的厌恶不会改变。
t_en: That's a touchy subject. I need to think more about it. But until I come to a conclusion, I won't change my distaste for it.

t_ch: 哎，会这么说也是因为…我对音乐的爱不会改变。
t_en: Look, I'm saying all this because... my love for music will never change.

t_ch: 你会尊重它们吗？
t_en: Will you show them respect?

t_ch: 会…吧？有什么不这么做的理由吗？我可没无聊到以欺负这些小东西为乐。
t_en: I will... perhaps? I mean, why not? I'm not so bored that I find pleasure in bullying those little creatures.

t_ch: 但这丝毫没法动摇我对自动乐团的质疑！
t_en: But that doesn't mean I won't criticize automated orchestras!

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.

t_ch: 我对你的态度，得取决于你的音乐品味。
t_en: My opinion of you depends on your taste in music.

t_ch: 就是这样，我知道你是酒吧的调饮师，聊天的能力肯定不错，但想用花言巧语来接近我是不可能的。我永远只会为美妙的旋律而沦陷。
t_en: That's the crux of it. I know you're a drinksmith and a skilled conversationalist, but flowery words won't impress me. Only a great melody will win me over.

t_ch: 自动乐团制造出来那些噪音不算是音乐啊，别想投机取巧！
t_en: And don't try cheap tricks! The noises coming from automated orchestras don't count as music!

t_ch: 都站到了舞台上，我们是不是应该说点什么？
t_en: You know, we're on the stage together, shouldn't we talk a little?

t_ch: 唉，为何沉重的命运总压在我身上，为何怪物不能停止互相伤害，非以戏耍同类为乐？
t_en: Ah, why must the great weight of destiny weigh so heavy on my shoulders? Why can't monsters stop harming each other and learn to take joy from playing together?

t_ch: 开心点呀，人家要和你表演的是甜甜的喜剧。
t_en: Cheer up! We're supposed to be performing a sweet comedic piece together!

t_ch: 至少在故事里，要表现得和人家更亲近点。这样台下的客人们才会感到幸福呢。
t_en: You have to act a little more intimate with me, at least in the story. That is the only way to make the audience happy.

t_ch: 我…我努力…努力试试。
t_en: I... I will try... my best...

t_ch: 嘻嘻，不用那么紧张啦，因为要演出的，本来就是我们的故事呀。
t_en: Hehe, don't be so nervous! This is our story we're performing, after all!

t_ch: 我会祝福你们的。
t_en: I wish you both well.

t_ch: 感谢您。希望下次人家也能给您带来幸福呀。
t_en: Thank you. I hope next time we can bring you happiness, too.

t_ch: 我才不会祝福你们呢。
t_en: I do not wish you well.

t_ch: …您是生气了嘛，那下次换人家来给您带来幸福呀。
t_en: ...Are you angry? Then next time allow me to bring you happiness.

t_ch: 唉，那就勉为其难的…和你共演吧，不准欺负我啊。
t_en: Ah, then I guess I have to try... I'll perform with you, but you're not allowed to bully me!

t_ch: 真的吗？人家好感动，那就赶快开始吧！
t_en: Really? Oh, I'm so touched! We have to begin immediately!

t_ch: 人家觉得，生活呀，就是充满了...无数像这样意义深远的邂逅。
t_en: I feel like, this life we live, it is so full... of countless unexpected and profound meetings. Like... like this one.

t_ch: 能遇见灵魂伴侣的概率，不过只有六分之一。
t_en: The chances of meeting your soulmate are but one in six.

t_ch: 我的人生目标，就是和你维持现状。
t_en: My dream in this life, is to be like this with you, forever.

t_ch: 它们的故事让我忍不住落泪，我最愿意为美好的爱情而欢呼了。
t_en: I can't help but cry watching their story. I will always let out a cheer for true love.

t_ch: 看啊，它们是如此般配，如此的令人歆羡。
t_en: Look, they are such a perfect match! I can't help but admire and envy them!

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)

t_ch: 属于我的那个她，我早已遇见，可是…她如今在哪呢？
t_en: My true love, I met her long ago, but... Where is she now?

t_ch: 想听听你对这家酒吧的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about this lounge.

t_ch: 我好爱这个地方，空气中都弥漫着甜腻的香味！
t_en: I love this place! It's got a sweet aroma in the air!

t_ch: 那位神秘酒保，和我的爱人有着相似的魅力，温柔，又是如此英姿飒爽！可惜我的心早已被她占据，容不下其余对象了。
t_en: That mysterious drinksmith, she has a similar charm and kindness as my beloved, yet she is heroic and bold, too! It is a shame that my heart has already been taken, and I have no space for another.

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.

t_ch: 你的意思…？不行，不可能，我的爱是如此坚定，绝不会发生偏移。
t_en: You mean... No, impossible, my love is resolute and will not be swayed!

t_ch: 我的心已被她夺走，即使给我调制那种特殊的饮料，即使你拿出安抚那群怪物的手段，也动摇不了我的信念！
t_en: She has stolen my heart, no specially mixed drink or secret monster-calming technique will shake my faith in love!

t_ch: 有趣的爱情故事总能让人轻易沉陷其中，尤其是…怪物间的亲密关系。
t_en: Interesting love stories are always so easy to get immersed in, especially... intimate relations between monsters.

t_ch: 您不觉得很有趣嘛？性格迥异的奇特生物，封闭的梦境空间，黑暗中逐渐发酵的复杂心绪…呀，光是谈到这几个关键词都忍不住开始浮想联翩了。
t_en: Don't you find it fascinating? Strange creatures with contrasting personalities, sealed inside a Dreamscape together as complex emotions start to ferment in the darkness... Ah, just these little tropes alone get my mind racing!

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)

t_ch: 可以啊，您想和我聊些什么呢？
t_en: Of course, what do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 想听听你对怪物们的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about monsters.

t_ch: 它们都是很…有意思的生命，对吧？梦境中的物件会走动，能像我们一样交流和思考，这本身就是件相当新奇的事，我喜欢观察它们的行为。
t_en: They are all very... interesting creatures, don't you think? The fact that objects in the Dreamscape can walk, communicate and think like us, this is such a novel concept. I enjoy observing their behavior.

t_ch: 更何况，酒吧里发生的事我都听说了，遭噩梦侵袭的被遗忘者，和不离不弃的照看者…这样的温柔的故事在哪都会受欢迎的。
t_en: What's more, I've heard about what happened in this lounge. The forgotten ones plagued by nightmares and the carer that refused to abandon them... A heartwarming story like this would be popular anywhere!

t_ch: 这家酒吧可真是适合取材的好地方，请允许我再多待段时间吧。
t_en: This lounge is a great place to get inspiration, please allow me to stay a while longer?

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.

t_ch: …在这样的氛围里，提出这样的问题，您的想法还真是出乎意料呢。
t_en: ...Quite the unexpected question to propose in a place with such an atmosphere.

t_ch: 可惜呀，要是故事的开头太过顺利，整本小说就失去吸引人读下去的动力了，所以请容许我暂且卖个关子，避开这个话题。
t_en: Unfortunately, if a story begins too smoothly, readers will lose interest in the story. So, I will skip this question for now.

t_ch: 现在我和您并肩坐在这里，共同度过了一段美好的时光，这就够了，不是吗？
t_en: Sitting side-by-side here, sharing in these beautiful moments, don't you think that's enough?

t_ch: …噢？这倒挺有意思的，怪物们的故事很适合作为新闻素材。
t_en: ...Hm? Now that's interesting, the story of these monsters would make great news articles.

t_ch: 「惊梦剧团」居然能与人类正常相处，甚至提供表演服务，看来那位传说中的调饮师确实挺厉害，下次请她来我们节目做特邀访谈嘉宾好了。
t_en: Not only can the Dreamjolt Troupe interact with humans, they even offer performances! It seems that the legendary drinksmith is indeed an impressive woman. I should invite her for a special feature interview on my program.

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)

t_ch: 我想想…时间应该还够，不过你得说快点，我是偷偷出来逛的。
t_en: Let me think... I probably don't have time but, speak quickly, I snuck out here to have a look around.

t_ch: 想听听你对舒翁的看法。
t_en: I want to know your thoughts on Siobhan.

t_ch: 舒翁？是酒吧的那位神秘调饮师对吧，关于她的消息，我多多少少听过点。
t_en: Siobhan? That's the name of the mysterious drinksmith, correct? I've heard a number of things about her.

t_ch: 她的身份可不简单，具体情况你自己去打听吧，反正是个在家族内部很有名望的人，没记错的话…应该是鸢尾花家系？可别说是我告诉你的啊。
t_en: Her background is complicated, if you want to know the specifics then you should ask around for yourself. Either way, I know that she holds a lot of renown within The Family... The Iris Family, if I remember rightly...? You cannot tell anyone that you heard this from me.

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.

t_ch: 你是这家酒吧新招来的调饮师？听说最近都是你在照顾那群怪物，效果挺好的，它们都很信任你，愿意把想法和你分享，看来你在沟通方面很有一套嘛。
t_en: Are you the new drinksmith here? I hear that it's you that has been looking after those monsters lately? I hear it's going well, and they trust you a lot, that they are willing to share their thoughts with you. It seems you're quite the adept communicator.

t_ch: 我会找人把这里的见闻写成报道，让更多观众都知道的，等着上电视吧。
t_en: I will send someone to write a report on what I've seen here and let our viewers know. Keep an eye on the TV!

t_ch: 嘿！刚才有五个苏乐达汽水瓶在空中飞！
t_en: Hey! There were five SoulGlad bottles flying in the air!

t_ch: 不准围观。这不是动物表演！
t_en: No spectators. This is not an animal show!

t_ch: 好吧，为了这家酒吧的人气考虑，稍微牺牲下也没什么。
t_en: Well, I guess I can make a compromise for the sake of the lounge's popularity.

t_ch: 你还有事情没告诉我。
t_en: There's something you haven't told me.

t_ch: 哼，是回忆里的那场真心话大冒险吧，我还以为你忘了。
t_en: Hmph, it's about the Truth or Dare game, right? I thought you had forgotten about it.

t_ch: 我可以告诉你当时的事，但你必须回答一个问题。
t_en: I can tell you what happened back then, but you need to answer me a question.

t_ch: 我接受。
t_en: Sounds fair.

t_ch: 这是我和舒翁的约定。她不在酒吧的时候，我会照顾那些怪物。
t_en: This is my agreement with Siobhan. When she's not around the lounge, I'll take care of the monsters.

t_ch: 所以，我有义务逗它们开心。
t_en: So, it's my job to keep them entertained.

t_ch: …后空翻喝汽水。这是我以前从电视节目里学来的技巧。
t_en: ...Drinking SoulGlad while doing backflips. Learned it from a TV show.

t_ch: 只要表演这个戏法，其他怪物都会笑出来。
t_en: Every time I pull off that trick, the other monsters will laugh out loud.

t_ch: 轮到你回答了。我想知道——如果舒翁没有提出委托，见到我们，你会怎么做？
t_en: Now, it's your turn. I'd like to know... what would you have done to us if Siobhan hadn't hired you?

t_ch: …进入战斗？
t_en: ...Get into a fight?

t_ch: …远远避开？
t_en: ...Keep a distance?

t_ch: …呼唤家族？
t_en: ...Call The Family?

t_ch: …什么都不做？
t_en: ...Do nothing at all?

t_ch: 但无论如何，你的选择里绝对不会有——「和这群怪物成为朋友」。
t_en: No matter what you answer, I doubt "become friends with these monsters" would've been on the list.

t_ch: 对你来说，我们不过是梦境里随处可见的游荡物品罢了。
t_en: To you, we're just wandering objects spread all over the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 没关系，我们本就不该对人类奢望过多。
t_en: It's fine. I know we shouldn't expect too much from humans.

t_ch: 就当我没问过。你继续去忙吧。
t_en: Let's pretend I didn't ask this question. Now, go back to your business.

t_ch: …你很喜欢打台球吗？
t_en: ...Do you enjoy playing billiards?

t_ch: 哼。计算，瞄准，出杆，简单的几何学。
t_en: Hmm, calculations, aiming, shots... simple geometry.

t_ch: 在充满变数的梦境里，这是仅剩的确定性。我喜欢不变的东西。
t_en: It's the only certainty left in this ever-changing Dreamscape. I appreciate things that remain unchanged.

t_ch: 你看起来很开心。
t_en: You seem to be enjoying yourself.

t_ch: 是吧。这家酒吧暂时保住了，我们的生活不会被打扰。这就是值得高兴的事。
t_en: Yeah, indeed. This lounge is safe for now, and our lives won't be disrupted. That's something to be happy about.

t_ch: 也祝你在这过得开心。我们的调饮师。
t_en: Enjoy your time here as well, our drinksmith.

t_ch: 看看这杆！嘿！
t_en: Look at this! Hey!

t_ch: 加到六个瓶子！放心，我肯定不会失手。
t_en: Make it six! No worries, I've got this.

t_ch: …奇怪，这只运输道具，我是不是在哪见过？感觉很熟悉。
t_en: ...Hmmm, have I ever seen this transportation thing before? It feels familiar.

t_ch: 算了！想这么多干嘛，相同用途的「美梦剧团」都长一个样，不需要专门去分辨！
t_en: Ah, forget it! Same-purpose Sweet Dreams Troupe members all look the same! Why bother distinguishing them?

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)

t_ch: 哟，您想找我聊聊，完全没问题！想看看我训练的物品吗？
t_en: Hey, if you'd like to talk, I'm all ears. Maybe you'd be interested in the props I've trained?

t_ch: 想听听你对这家酒吧的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about this lounge.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，要我说，这可真是个傻地方！我早就知道有几位驯物师同僚，把道具当作自己的伙伴，又是照顾又是保护的，没想到就是这里。
t_en: You know what? This place is filled with morons! I've heard that some of my fellow Props Masters treat props like companions, but I didn't know they're living here.

t_ch: 我听说这些怪物都被训练得服服帖帖，才打算过来取经。但这哪是驯物啊，简直都把它们供起来了，还给道具调饮料，这不是搞笑吗？
t_en: I heard those monsters are incredibly obedient, so I came to see how it's done. But what do I find? This is not prop-taming at all! Those fools treat these monsters like their bosses and even mix drinks for them! It's downright hilarious!

t_ch: 不听话的道具就该用鞭子抽！不管什么情绪，给我卖命工作就对了！
t_en: Disobedient items deserve a good whipping! I don't care about their moods! They're supposed to work hard for me!

t_ch: 就算你不干，有的是其它道具来干！
t_en: And even if some of them don't feel like working, I have plenty of substitutes!

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.

t_ch: 哎呦，客人，我哪敢对您有看法啊。您能喜欢道具表演就是我的荣幸了。
t_en: Oh, dear guest! How dare I have complaints about you? I'm honored that you're interested in my item show.

t_ch: …现在要看吗？这里的环境悠闲又清净，正适合看点好玩的节目来打发时间！
t_en: ...So, would you like to enjoy the show now? A little entertainment in this quiet joint would be the perfect way to kill some time!

t_ch: 观看驯物表演。
t_en: I'll watch the prop-taming show.

t_ch: 您稍等，我这就来准备。
t_en: Just a moment. It'll start soon.

t_ch: …还是算了吧。
t_en: ...Perhaps not.

t_ch: 别急着拒绝啊，我先给您演示看看。
t_en: Don't be so quick to say "no." Let me show you how it's done.

t_ch: 这位骄傲的驯物师才刚从口袋里掏出长鞭，一个汽水瓶盖就径直砸到了他的头上。
t_en: Just as the proud props master reaches for his whip, a soda bottle cap smacks him right on the head.

t_ch: 哇！疼疼疼疼疼疼疼——是谁干的！
t_en: Ouch! It hurts!! Who the heck did that!?

t_ch: 抱歉，我扔偏了。
t_en: My bad, I missed.

t_ch: 没用的东西！要是我管理的道具，非得拿鞭子把你抽到服…哎呀！！！
t_en: You useless piece of junk! If I were your master, this whip would... Ouch!!!

t_ch: 抱歉，我又扔偏了。
t_en: My bad, I missed again.

t_ch: 只要你坐在那，我就总是忍不住想瞄准你的头。
t_en: As long as you're sitting there, I can't resist aiming for your head.

t_ch: 你…我去向你的主人告状！给我等着瞧！我就不信还处理不了你一个道具了！
t_en: You... I'll tell your master! Just you wait! There will be consequences!
(Meow...)
(Meow!)

t_ch: 它看起来很喜欢这个地方。
t_en: It seems to be fond of this place.

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)
(Meow?)

t_ch: 想听听你对这些饮料的看法。
t_en: It seems to be asking for your thoughts on the drinks.
(Meow!)

t_ch: 它似乎对你调制的饮料很感兴趣！
t_en: It seems interested in the drinks you've mixed!
(Meow...)

t_ch: 它似乎很遗憾自己没能喝到饮料……
t_en: It seems to regret not getting a drink...
(Meow?)

t_ch: 它似乎在询问饮料能打包带走吗？
t_en: It seems to be asking if it can pack the drinks and take them away.
(Meow.)

t_ch: 它跳下了椅子，似乎不想再聊了。
t_en: It jumps off the chair and doesn't seem interested in talking anymore.

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.
(...Meow?)

t_ch: 怎么回事？在你提出问题的一瞬间，一名智械女性突然出现在了眼前！
t_en: What's going on? The moment you ask the question, an Intellitron girl pops out of nowhere!

t_ch: …你是什么意思？
t_en: ...What do you mean?

t_ch: 先警告你一句，别对那些可爱的小动物动歪心思。它们都很纯粹，离远点，不准随便靠近，免得沾染上人的臭味。
t_en: Consider yourself warned — don't get any funny ideas about those cute little animals. They are pure, and it's best to keep your distance, or you will taint them with human stink.

t_ch: 至于你…这家酒吧的事，我都打听过了，你像是个会平等对待动物的人，但就算如此，我还是没法相信你。
t_en: As for you... I've heard about this lounge. Even if you seem like someone who treats animals the same as humans, I'm not trusting you completely.

t_ch: 生命形式的差别不能轻易抹平，理解只是空谈！
t_en: Differences in life forms can't be easily ignored, and mutual understanding is merely empty talk!

t_ch: 安抚心灵的神奇饮品，要是能在梦境里推广开来该有多好啊。
t_en: A magical drink that soothes the soul. It'd be wonderful if it could spread far and wide in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 不过…所有负面情绪都能被轻易消除的话，我这样的梦境鼓励师还有什么用呢？
t_en: However... if all the negative emotions could be easily erased like this, who would need a Dreamscape Motivator like me anymore?

t_ch: （进行搭讪）
t_en: (Chat)

t_ch: 您好，是需要咨询吗？这里很安静，正适合进行深入谈话。
t_en: Greetings, do you need counseling? It's quiet here, perfect for an in-depth conversation.

t_ch: 想听听你对情绪的看法。
t_en: I'd like to hear your thoughts on emotions.

t_ch: 情绪是种很奇妙的存在，它没有实体，无法被清晰观测到。它是活着的某种状态，同样可以是错觉。情绪能被有意识地制造，也能被强行改变或消除。
t_en: Emotions are fascinating. They have no tangible form and can't be clearly observed. They are certain states of being alive, and they can be both real and illusory. They can be consciously created, altered, or even eliminated.

t_ch: 但对「美梦剧团」而言，情绪能决定它们的本性。它们的生命附属于梦境，不会生病或衰老，不受时间的控制，却会因情绪转变而发生根本变化。
t_en: However, for the Sweet Dreams Troupe, their emotions determine their very nature. Their lives stem from the Dreamscape, and they won't fall ill or age. However, their essence can change dramatically due to shifts in emotions.

t_ch: 很不可思议吧？所以，听说这家酒吧的故事后，我很好奇。
t_en: It's incredible, isn't it? That's why I became curious after hearing the story of this lounge.

t_ch: …究竟是什么样的力量在保护着怪物们，不被噩梦彻底侵蚀呢？
t_en: ...I wonder, what powers have been protecting the monsters from being completely corrupted by the Nightmare?

t_ch: 想听听你对我的看法。
t_en: I want to know what you think about me.

t_ch: …您？可、可…我是梦境鼓励师，您是我的客户。这样的关系…也太危险了啊。
t_en: ...You? B—But I... I'm a Dreamscape Motivator, and you're my client. Such a relationship... would be far too dangerous.

t_ch: 不行不行，虽说我的职责是帮助您消除烦恼，但这也并不意味着……
t_en: No, no, no, even though my duty is to help you alleviate your worries, it doesn't mean...

t_ch: 深呼吸…抱歉，我可能误解了您的意思。真要说，我认为您是位极为优秀的心理咨询师，这些「惊梦剧团」的情绪问题不是能轻易解决的，您处理得很漂亮。
t_en: *gasp* ...I'm sorry. I may have misunderstood you. To be honest, I think you're an exceptional counselor. The emotional issues of the Dreamjolt Troupe members are not easily resolved, but you've handled them impressively.

t_ch: 你没有误解。
t_en: No, you didn't misunderstand.

t_ch: 咦？！…对、对不起！我需要暂时离开，来好好平复我的情绪！
t_en: Huh!?... I... I'm sorry! I need to take some time away to calm down my emotions!

t_ch: 你也可以找我咨询。
t_en: You can also ask me for counseling.

t_ch: 哈哈，虽说我确实也有些情绪问题，不过还没到必须麻烦其他人的程度。有机会的话，还是让我尝尝您调制的饮料吧。
t_en: Haha, I admit that I may have some emotional issues too, but it's not to the extent that I need to bother others. I'll just try one of your drinks.

t_ch: 你尝试向眼前的顾客搭话，但对方似乎沉浸在酒吧的悠闲氛围中，并未注意到你。
t_en: You attempt to engage in conversation with the customer before you, but they seem immersed in the leisurely ambiance of the lounge, oblivious to your presence.

t_ch: 真高兴见到你，星穹列车的开拓者。
t_en: Pleasure to meet you, Trailblazer of the Astral Express.

t_ch: 我现在这么出名了？
t_en: Am I that famous now?

t_ch: 需要签名吗？今天不行…
t_en: Are you looking for an autograph? Not today...

t_ch: 看来情报不错。你的说话风格确实…幽默。
t_en: It seems my intel was spot on. You do have a rather... humorous manner of speaking.

t_ch: 小时候，我总是缠着父辈们给我讲无名客们的银河冒险故事。你们简直是我的童年偶像…真没想到，我现在居然有机会和你们面对面聊天。
t_en: When I was a kid, I kept asking folks from my parents' generation to tell me tales of the Nameless' cosmic adventures. You were basically my childhood heroes... I never imagined I'd be able to speak to you face-to-face like this.

t_ch: 哎…可惜啊，家族需要我，我还有任务在身。要不然，我真想辞了这个工作，和你们星穹列车一起旅行。
t_en: Ah... Unfortunately, The Family needs me. I still have a mission to do. If it weren't for that, I'd leave this job in a heartbeat and travel with you on the Astral Express.

t_ch: 欢迎上车！热烈欢迎！
t_en: You'd be welcome aboard!

t_ch: 呵，看来是真货——恭喜，你的嫌疑被洗清了。
t_en: Ha, looks like you are the real deal — Congratulations, you are no longer under suspicion.

t_ch: 我做不了主，得问问列车长。
t_en: I can't call the shots. You'll have to ask the conductor.

t_ch: 列车长…呵，看来是真货——恭喜，你的嫌疑被洗清了。
t_en: The conductor... Ha, looks like you are the real deal — Congratulations, you are no longer under suspicion.

t_ch: 你诈我？
t_en: Did you just trick me?

t_ch: 什么嫌疑？你的任务就是这个？
t_en: What suspicion? Is this your mission?

t_ch: 呵呵，特殊时期，还请多多担待。最近风头紧，现在猎犬的鼻子比以往任何时候都灵敏。
t_en: Hehe. These are special times, so please bear with me. Things have been tense recently. The Hounds' noses have been sharper than ever.

t_ch: 别说傻话，别做傻事，你就不会惹上麻烦。也祝你在匹诺康尼玩得愉快。
t_en: There won't be any trouble so long as you don't say or do anything foolish. I hope you enjoy your time in Penacony.

t_ch: 有兴趣聊聊吗？
t_en: Wanna chat?

t_ch: 当然。正好给我打掩护，尽管聊吧。
t_en: You bet. That'll be the perfect cover. Chat to your heart's content.

t_ch: 你在做什么？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 度假，旅游，约会，享受生活，追杀会飞的皮皮西…哪条比较可信？
t_en: Resorts, travel, dates, enjoying life, or hunting down a Pepeshi who can fly... Which one is more believable?

t_ch: 我为这个问题准备了一百个答案，但都不太适合你。不过，既然你是大剧院事件的亲历者…我倒是可以稍微和你聊聊。
t_en: I've prepared a hundred answers to this question, but none of them would really suit you. That said, I suppose I could talk with you a bit... since you experienced the Grand Theater incident firsthand.

t_ch: 「秩序」——或者说橡木家系——相信你们已经照过面了。这群人背叛了神主，必然要受到相应的惩罚。但他们仍在美梦中流窜，而我们正在…有序收网。
t_en: I trust you have already encountered Order — or should I say the Oak Family. They betrayed the Great One and must be punished accordingly. However, they are still scuttling somewhere in the dreamscape, while we are in the process of... tightening the surrounding net one step at a time.

t_ch: 质疑神主，控制欲过强，这都是受「秩序」影响的表现。如果你身边有这种人，请务必告诉我，报酬管够。
t_en: Questioning the Great One and having an overwhelming desire for control are all signs of Order's influence. If anyone around you exhibits those signs, please let me know. The reward will be worth your while.

t_ch: 关于「秩序」。
t_en: About Order.

t_ch: 神主吸收了太一的「秩序」，相对的，那位陨落的星神也在我等谐乐中留下了杂音。就像一种跨越时空的诅咒，久久盘桓在家族头上。
t_en: The Great One absorbed Ena of Order. Consequently, that fallen Aeon left behind a dissonance in our harmonious music. It is like some kind of curse that transcends time and space and has hung over The Family's head for ages.

t_ch: 虽然老东西们从不把小喽啰放在眼里，但放任不管也会滋生重疾…就像现在。想要「同谐」伟力长存，就必须把旧病的残渣清除干净。
t_en: Though those old fools never gave their underlings any thought, ignoring them will cause the wound to fester... just like it is now. If the Harmony's power is to remain eternal, then every last trace of that old wound must be excised.

t_ch: 更多的嘛…我就不能说了。刚才这段小故事可是特殊服务，别外传。
t_en: Anything beyond that... I can't tell you. That little story was an exclusive service. Don't tell anyone else.

t_ch: 我猜这不是你的真面目…
t_en: I guess this isn't who you really are...

t_ch: 当然。见人说人话，见鬼说鬼话，这是猎犬特工的必备素质。
t_en: Of course not. Changing your personality to fit the person you're with is a trait a Hound agent must have.

t_ch: 别嫌弃我不够真诚…你肯定不会希望我以对待叛徒的态度来对待你的。
t_en: Don't be disappointed that I'm not honest enough... I'm positive you wouldn't want me to handle you with the same attitude I come at traitors with.

t_ch: 没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 期待与你的再会。
t_en: I look forward to meeting you again.

t_ch: 真高兴有你陪我…一个人在这儿盯梢，我快闷死了。
t_en: I'm happy to have you keep me company... I would have been bored to tears scouting here all alone.

t_ch: 黑爪女士笑盈盈地看着你，不变的嘴角弧度让你脊背发凉。
t_en: Lady Black Claw looks at you pleasantly, but the way the arc of her lips is constantly frozen in the same smile sends shivers down your spine.

t_ch: 聊点什么好呢…啊，有了——听说过「安谧的时刻」么？有机会的话，我可以带你去逛逛。参观参观噩梦边境的监牢，见识各种有趣的…人。
t_en: What else could we talk about... Ah, I know. Have you heard of the Moment of Serenity? If we have the opportunity, I can take you to check it out. You can see the jail on the Nightmare's edge and meet some interesting... people.

t_ch: 有个家伙蹲了两个琥珀纪的号子，猜猜为什么？那家伙满嘴秽语，侮辱家族…警告无果，我们只好请他换个地头生活，免得扰民。
t_en: There was this guy who's been in jail for two Amber Eras. Can you guess why? He was constantly spouting off obscenities and insulting The Family... He paid no heed to our warnings, so our only option was to ask him to live somewhere else to avoid riling people up.

t_ch: 他现在还保持着每天辱骂家族的习惯呢，大不敬发言记录了整整十册。他可能觉得自己能骂死我们吧…呵呵。
t_en: Even now he still insults The Family every day. His disrespectful tirades filled at least ten books. Maybe he's under the impression that he can insult us to death... Hehe.

t_ch: 对了，还有个奇才——她宣称创造了一种完美的社会形式，到处推销，甚至偷偷溜进大剧院，准备趁剧目的中场休息阶段上台演说……
t_en: Oh, yeah. Then there's this other special person. She declared that she created a perfect form of society and was pushing the idea wherever she went. She even snuck into the Grand Theater and planned to take to the stage during intermission to give her spiel...

t_ch: 还有，我最喜欢的一位：那家伙只因为想拍恐怖片，硬是驯服了三十九只迷因。结果那群张牙舞爪的小家伙跑到「蓝调的时刻」大闹了一场…这下成沉浸式恐怖电影了。
t_en: Then there's my favorite. He wanted to shoot a horror film so badly that he tamed and trained <unbreak>39</unbreak> memes. So, this fierce-looking group headed to the Blue Hour and caused a big scene... They truly made an immersive horror film.

t_ch: 觉得他们都挺无害的，对吧？至于那些有害的…呵呵，在美梦里还是不聊这个了。
t_en: You think they were harmless, right? Well, as for those that weren't so harmless... Hehe, we won't chat about that in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 有趣的小把戏…如果我会你这种手段，审问应该能轻松不少。
t_en: Neat little trick there... If I could use that tactic of yours, my interrogations would probably be way easier.

t_ch: 黑爪女士自在地吹了个口哨，完全看不出悲伤的样子。她到底是怎么察觉到「钟表把戏」的？
t_en: Lady Black Claw whistles casually. You can't see a hint of sadness in her. How could she have detected the Clockwork?

t_ch: 来聊聊犯事的家伙们吧。正好，前段日子抓了个筑梦师，足够作为谈资。
t_en: Let's chat about some people who've committed crimes. It just so happens a Dreamweaver got arrested a few days ago. That should be a decent topic of conversation.

t_ch: 有大剧院事件在先，那些潜伏已久的叛徒们纷纷闻风而动。这个筑梦师嘛，就是其中一员。
t_en: There was the Grand Theater incident that happened before. Those traitors who had been lurking in the shadows for so long caught wind of what was going on and took action. The Dreamweaver was one of them.

t_ch: 他往所在的筑梦工事里创造了一枚自毁装置——别问，这当然不合法——他想以此来要挟家族，让我们为他划分出一块「秩序」的试验田，自己当国王，容纳所有信徒居住。
t_en: He created a self-destruction device in the dream construction project he belonged to — yes, that's illegal, I know — He wanted to use the explosive to extort The Family so that we'd give him a testing field for Order. He wanted a place where he could be king and get all the believers to live there.

t_ch: 可惜，他还没来得及要挟，计划就破产了。知道他怎么暴露的吗？被他的「战友」们卖了：他们觉得那人追求的东西根本不是「秩序」，所以直接找到了我们。
t_en: Unfortunately for him, he never got the chance to squeeze The Family. His plan fell apart. Do you know how his little scheme got exposed? His "allies" sold him out. They felt he wasn't after Order at all, so they approached us directly.

t_ch: 这群人…明明信奉一尊「秩序」，却自以为自己的理想才是唯一的「秩序」。呵呵，可怜虫。
t_en: This group... They believe in the same Order, and yet they assume that their own ideal is the only Order out there. They're so pitiful.

t_ch: 这点小伎俩…想要动摇「同谐」的洪流，还是有点不自量力了。
t_en: They tried to divert the torrent that is Harmony... with that little trick. That was a serious overestimation of their strength.

t_ch: 黑爪女士露出深邃的笑容，似乎在期待你会如何回应。「钟表把戏」似乎没有产生作用…「家人」的实力恐怖如斯。
t_en: Lady Black Claw has this profound smile on her face as if in anticipation of how you are going to react. It looks like Clockwork isn't taking effect. The strength of "Family members" sure is terrifying.

t_ch: 我的音级远高于那些普通的家人。虽然我不知道你的小把戏来自哪道命途的力量…但无论如何，这种挠痒痒似的把戏，在我身上只会被「同谐」的力量「啪」地弹开。
t_en: My scale degree goes far beyond the average Family member. I may not know what Path your little trick drew power from, but... that trifling ability merely bounces off of me thanks to the power of Harmony.

t_ch: 想当年，有个忆者和我大战了三百回合，愣是没撼动我的记忆半分，最后当场与我义结金兰…名字暂时没编好，你只用知道姐姐我很厉害就行了。
t_en: It makes me think of that one time when a Memokeeper fought me for three hundred rounds and shockingly couldn't even disturb a single bit of my memory. In the end, the Memokeeper and I became sworn sisters... I haven't made up a name yet, but all you need to know is that I am incredible.

t_ch: 「同谐」的合唱既给予我们力量，也会淡化我们的情感，强烈的情绪例外。比如，和你聊天就很开心……
t_en: Harmony's united choir imbues us with strength but also dilutes our emotions. Except for strong emotions, that is. For example, how I am happy to be speaking with you...

t_ch: 来！这笔钱就当是姐姐给你的朋友费，在匹诺康尼狠狠地消费吧！
t_en: Here! Take this money. Think of it as some cash from a friend. Go out and spend big while you are here in Penacony!

t_ch: 黑爪女士表达愤怒的方式还真是…与众不同。
t_en: The way Lady Black Claw expresses anger is... certainly unique.

t_ch: 我的音级远高于那些普通的家人。虽然我不知道你的小把戏来自哪道命途的力量…但无论如何，这种挠痒痒似的把戏，在我身上只会被「同谐」的力量「啪」地弹开。
t_en: My scale degree goes far beyond the average Family member. I may not know what Path your little trick drew power from, but... that trifling ability merely bounces off of me thanks to the power of Harmony.

t_ch: 想当年，有个忆者和我大战了三百回合，愣是没撼动我的记忆半分，最后当场与我义结金兰…名字暂时没编好，你只用知道姐姐我很厉害就行了。
t_en: It makes me think of that one time when a Memokeeper fought me for three hundred rounds and shockingly couldn't even disturb a single bit of my memory. In the end, the Memokeeper and I became sworn sisters... I haven't made up a name yet, but all you need to know is that I am incredible.

t_ch: 嘘、嘘——「秩序」的残秽们又有新动静了。
t_en: Shhhh. The remnant of Order is up to something again.

t_ch: 黑爪女士的视线穿过你，落到一个遥远的地方…可那里根本没有人。
t_en: Lady Black Claw's gaze goes right past you and is locked on a distant spot... But no one's there.

t_ch: 想要借「秩序」来实现自己私欲的，往往没法掀起太大的波澜。真正可怕的，是那些自以为在救赎世人的家伙。
t_en: If these people are using Order to achieve their own desires, then they can never amount to anything. The truly scary ones are those who think they are saving the world.
